frame_analyses.description: Beginning to load the needle into the needle holder.
Adjusting needle position between needle holders.
Needle penetrates one mark on the Penrose drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Placement near the mark with slight deviation.

frame_analyses.description: Needle exits through the second mark.
First throw of the surgeon's knot being tied.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Executed with right hand, appears stable.

frame_analyses.description: Second throw being tied, switching hands.
Third throw being tied, switching hands again.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper hand switch noted, knot looks secure.

frame_analyses.description: Preparing to cut the suture ends.
Suture ends are being cut, completing the task.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Ends cut neatly, task appears concluded.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Slit properly closed, no visible gaps.

technique_summary: The suture placement was done with moderate accuracy, and the knot tying showed good technique with secure results. Hand switching was correctly executed.
improvement_suggestions: Focus on more precise entry and exit points during suture placement.
Improve speed while maintaining accuracy to reduce completion time.
strengths: Knot tying technique is strong and consistent.
Hand switching was performed effectively between throws.
